codeReview.summary: The product detail page implementation is solid and covers all required functionality: breadcrumb navigation (Home > Shop > Product Name), two-column layout with image gallery and buying panel, subscription/one-time toggle with 15% savings, quantity selector with +/- buttons, ADD TO CART button that calls CartContext.addItem() and opens the cart slide-in, accordion sections for Benefits/How to Use/Ingredients, trust badges row (Chef Crafted, Nutrient Dense, Locally Sourced), and related products carousel. The code follows project conventions well — proper TypeScript types, Tailwind utility classes, zero border-radius, correct component decomposition (server page + client PDPContent), accessibility attributes (aria-label, aria-pressed on thumbnails, breadcrumb nav landmark), and the design system colors/spacing. The CartContext was cleanly extended with isCartOpen/openCart/closeCart to allow PDP to trigger the cart slide-in, and the Header was updated to use these shared state values instead of local state. No blocking issues found.

issues.description: Subscription state is not passed to addItem(). The CartItem type only has {product, quantity} — no isSubscription flag. When a user selects 'Subscribe & Save 15%' and clicks ADD TO CART, the subscription choice is lost and the cart/checkout will show the full price ($18.00) instead of the subscription price ($15.30). This is a data model gap that will need addressing in the cart-page feature or a future iteration.

Thumbnail buttons use array index as key (key={index}). Since product images are a static array that doesn't reorder, this is functionally fine and won't cause rendering bugs, but using the image URL as key would be more semantically correct.

Initial subscription state defaults to false (One-Time Purchase selected). The feature description says 'subscription shows Subscribe & Save 15% with lower price' suggesting subscription might be the default highlight, but the current default of one-time purchase is a reasonable UX choice matching Sakara's pattern.

TrustBadges is a server component (no 'use client' directive) which is correct since it has no interactivity. However, the grid layout uses grid-cols-3 without a responsive fallback — on very narrow screens (<375px), the 3-column grid may compress the text. A `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-3` breakpoint would improve ultra-narrow viewport handling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not run `pnpm build` as part of verification — only ran `npx tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm lint`. The SKILL.md procedure step 4 says 'Verify with TypeScript and Lint' which the worker followed, but step 5 (agent-browser verification) was done without a build step. The AGENTS.md Testing section lists `pnpm build` as the first validation command. Consider clarifying in the skill whether build is required or optional before visual verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification.commandsRun shows only tsc and lint, no pnpm build. AGENTS.md Testing section: 'pnpm build - Verify the app builds without errors' is listed first.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CartItem type does not support subscription pricing. This is a cross-cutting data model issue — any feature that adds items to cart with subscription pricing will face this same gap. AGENTS.md and architecture.md mention CartContext provides addItem/removeItem/updateQuantity but don't document whether subscription state should be tracked in cart items.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/lib/types.ts:124-127 — CartItem has only {product, quantity}. BuyingPanel.tsx:30 calls addItem(product, quantity) losing the isSubscription state. Cart subtotal at cart-context.tsx:111 always uses product.price.

summary: The product-detail-page feature passes review. The implementation is thorough, well-structured, and covers all 6 required elements (breadcrumb, image gallery with thumbnails, buying panel with subscription toggle/quantity/add-to-cart, accordions, trust badges, related products carousel). The code follows project conventions (TypeScript strict, Tailwind utilities, zero border-radius, proper component architecture with server/client split). The CartContext was cleanly extended to support opening the cart slide-in from the PDP. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) subscription state is not persisted to the cart data model, and (2) trust badges grid could benefit from a responsive breakpoint on ultra-narrow screens. No blocking issues.
